trigger: Skipped skill prompts, wrote throwaway inline Plugin API code for 488 ops; correct but burned excessive tokens and bypassed tested tooling.
lesson: Always use the existing pipeline scripts (audit/resolve/bulk-update/token-lookup) — never write ad-hoc inline Plugin API code for tree walks or bulk writes.

trigger: Bound 4 corners with 4 individual setBoundVariable calls — slow.
lesson: setBoundVariable('cornerRadius', v) binds all 4 corners in one call — ~5x faster than per-corner.

trigger: Fixed setup cost per mcp_figma_use_figma call ~7.5s (getNodeByIdAsync ~1.8s + 4x parallel getVariableByIdAsync ~5.6s).
lesson: Amortise setup cost over as many nodes as possible in one use_figma call (308 nodes in one call/78s is fine). Avoid many small calls.

trigger: getLocalVariablesAsync returns 514 vars — slow.
lesson: For NV-only writes with known IDs, skip getLocalVariablesAsync; use getVariableByIdAsync via Promise.all. Cache the name→id map per fileKey+version.

trigger: figma.getLocalEffectStyles() (sync) returned empty/stale in some Plugin API contexts, giving a false ∅ in QA.
lesson: Always use the async read APIs (getLocalEffectStylesAsync, getLocalVariablesAsync). Sync style/variable reads can be stale.

trigger: Needed to bind fills on icon VECTORs inside INSTANCE children.
lesson: setBoundVariableForPaint works on nested instance-override nodes — traverse comp.children recursively; no getNodeByIdAsync per node needed.

trigger: Ordering of textStyleId vs fill on TEXT nodes.
lesson: textStyleId assignment on TEXT nodes is synchronous and works on instance-override TEXT nodes; assign before fill to avoid ordering issues.

trigger: 'The user cancelled the tool call' was misread as a Figma/network hang.
lesson: A cancelled tool call means the user clicked Cancel in the approval dialog, not a network hang. Run a 2-line smoke test to prove connectivity before bulk writes.

trigger: Sync figma.getNodeById can fail to resolve inner node IDs mid-script.
lesson: Always use figma.getNodeByIdAsync; obtain inner node refs via tree traversal, never getNodeById in a loop.

trigger: Planning a future swap from the local .hermes store to a real Hermes install.
lesson: Real Hermes contracts: state=SessionDB (~/.hermes/state.db) with a state_meta k/v table — map state.write(runId) -> set_meta('run:<runId>', json), same pattern as goal:<id>. A run=kanban_db.Run (claim->complete|blocked|failed|timeout|reclaimed). The ODIN loop=goals.py Ralph loop (status active|paused|done|cleared, judge verdict done|continue). Skills(SKILL.md, loaded on demand) vs Memory(compact, injected). manifest.json=a skill-bundle {name,description,skills[],instruction}. Keep these analogies intact.

trigger: FDS-Card audit: Padding=True uses TS key `spacing` (maps to itemSpacing/gap) instead of `verticalPadding`/`horizontalPadding`; also uses generic fds-spacing-200 instead of the component-specific fds-spacing-const-container-card.
lesson: For card/container components: check that TS uses `verticalPadding`/`horizontalPadding` keys (not `spacing`) for padding insets. Prefer fds-spacing-const-container-card over fds-spacing-200 for card internal padding.

ruleProposal.diff: # Consider adding a rule: FDS-Card Padding=True -> key verticalPadding + horizontalPadding -> spacing.fds-spacing-const.container.fds-spacing-const-container-card

trigger: COMPONENT_SET with zero GROUPs and all COMPONENT children already VERTICAL AL.
lesson: When a COMPONENT_SET scan returns zero GROUPs and all COMPONENT children have non-NONE layoutMode, classify the run property-only: emit only sizing/slot-AL property corrections, no structural OPS. Note: Figma 2025+ SLOT nodes inside a COMPONENT are writable (unlike INSTANCE internals).

trigger: FDS-Card audit: TS-only draft, wrong border token on surface theme, generic padding token.
lesson: For card/container components: (1) padding insets should use verticalPadding/horizontalPadding keys, not `spacing` (which maps to itemSpacing); prefer fds-spacing-const-container-card over generic fds-spacing-200. (2) Watch for copy-paste border-token errors across themes (surface variants carrying fds-on-alternate-surface-ulow). (3) var.fds.* TS prefix signals NV binding is intended but may be unapplied (varMap empty).

trigger: NV names searched as fds/fds-surface-variant but actual Figma names are var/fds/fds-surface-variant — file uses var/ prefix namespace.
lesson: In this Figma file (Ahvbwk0dUHeHazrQX2XtGd), all NV variable names carry a var/ prefix. Audit shorthand fds/fds-* must be looked up as var/fds/fds-* in getLocalVariablesAsync. Always verify by running a discovery pass before the write pass on a new file.

ruleProposal.diff: # Add note: this file's NV names use var/ prefix (e.g. var/fds/fds-surface-variant, not fds/fds-surface-variant). Verify with discovery pass before write pass.

trigger: bulk-update.figma.js nv-type uses setBoundVariable(prop,v) which does not work for fills/strokes (paint arrays require setBoundVariableForPaint). Also has no ts-strip type.
lesson: bulk-update.figma.js nv-type is scalar-props-only (cornerRadius, strokeWeight, padding…). For fills/strokes NV binding use a targeted script with setBoundVariableForPaint. For TS key removal, setSharedPluginData(ns, key, '') strips the key. Document this gap in the script header.

trigger: Audit shorthand fds/fds-* did not match the actual Figma variable names, which carry a var/ prefix (var/fds/fds-*). First write on a new file failed to resolve until a getLocalVariablesAsync discovery pass.
lesson: On the FIRST NV write to any new fileKey, run a getLocalVariablesAsync discovery pass to capture the EXACT variable names before binding — do not assume the dot->slash shorthand equals the Figma variable name (this file prefixes all with var/). Cache the name->VariableID map per fileKey+version.

trigger: fds-collapsible on-alternate-surface Stroke=Hidden variants (78071/78080) carried a borderColor TS token while the on-surface Hidden equivalents had none — cross-theme copy-paste artifact.
lesson: When auditing multi-theme variant sets, diff the token keys of Hidden/no-stroke variants across themes; a borderColor present on one theme but absent on the matching variant of another theme is a copy-paste artifact — strip it rather than binding NV to it.

trigger: SAGA subagent could not download Figma MCP asset URLs (no terminal tool in its context) and hand-authored approximate SVGs; ODIN later curled the real assets.
lesson: When dispatching SAGA as a subagent, ODIN should download the Figma MCP asset SVG/PNG URLs (from get_design_context) via terminal and place them in the component assets/ folder BEFORE or right after SAGA writes markup — subagents may lack a terminal to fetch remote assets. Keep SAGA filenames stable so refs survive the swap.

trigger: Sort.Overlay audit: effectStyleId was present on all COMPONENT nodes (TS boxShadow key matched), but the standard audit-resolve-digest.figma.js does not capture effectStyleId — it only captures fillStyleId. A supplemental elevation probe was required.
lesson: effectStyleId is not captured by audit-resolve-digest.figma.js (only fillStyleId is). For components with drop-shadow/elevation, always run a supplemental getStyleByIdAsync probe. In Figma there are 0 NV variables for effects — elevation is managed via effectStyleId only, not boundVariables. Reporting effectStyleId present = elevation IS bound natively.

trigger: Figma MCP asset export for the check icon arrived with preserveAspectRatio="none" + width/height=100%, which stretches a non-square icon (viewBox 0 0 11.7267 8.94).
lesson: Figma MCP icon/SVG exports often carry preserveAspectRatio="none" and width/height=100%. Before placing in assets/, rewrite to the intrinsic viewBox with preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" and explicit intrinsic width/height; render in a fixed-aspect box (object-fit:contain) so the icon is never stretched.

trigger: FDS-Badge Status type: TS fill uses var.fds.fds-*-shade tokens (type: color, linear-gradient) which bind via fillStyleId (paint style), NOT fills NV variable. NV fills array contains the two ref/system/*50+*60 gradient stops.
lesson: fds-*-shade tokens are linear-gradient color tokens; in Figma they create a paint style assigned via fillStyleId (not boundVariables.fills). hasFillNV() correctly returns true due to fillStyleId presence. The two-entry NV fills array (ref/system/*60 + ref/system/*50) are the gradient stops. No issue — this is the correct binding path for gradient fills.

ruleProposal.diff: # Add fds-success-shade, fds-info-shade, fds-alert-shade, fds-error-shade to Color section as type:color linear-gradient tokens (gradient paint styles, not NV variables)

trigger: FDS-Badge Content Type=Blank variants: all TS+NV tokens present (same 8 TS keys, same 14 NV props as other content types), zero children.
lesson: For FDS-Badge, Blank content type variants are dot indicators with full structural token coverage but empty children[] — they share the same token schema as Numerical/Text variants. Do not flag missing typography/fill tokens on Blank variants.

trigger: FDS-Badge Sportsbook/Bet status/Won: fill NV = var/fds/fds-sb-state-won but text fill NV = var/fds/fds-sb-on-state-win (win vs won suffix mismatch).
lesson: In this Figma file, the Sportsbook Bet status Won NV variable pair has asymmetric naming: fds-sb-state-won (fill) vs fds-sb-on-state-win (on-fill). This is an established variable name — do not attempt to rename during token writes, but flag for designer review when Sportsbook tokens are audited.

trigger: FDS-Badge audit: fds-*-shade tokens (success/info/alert/error) are gradient paint styles bound via fillStyleId (dual ref/system/*50+*60 NV stops), not a single fills NV; also absent from token-registry.md though present in ts-core-fabric.json.
lesson: fds-*-shade status tokens are linear-gradient paint styles bound via fillStyleId, not a scalar fills NV — hasFillNV via fillStyleId is correct, do not flag as MISSING_NV_FILL. Add fds-success-shade/info-shade/alert-shade/error-shade to token-registry.md.

ruleProposal.diff: # Add 4 gradient status tokens: fds-success-shade, fds-info-shade, fds-alert-shade, fds-error-shade (gradient paint styles, bound via fillStyleId, stops ref/system/<status>/50+60)

trigger: FDS-Badge Blank content-type variants have empty children[] but carry the full 8 TS keys + 14 NV props.
lesson: Blank content-type variants are fully tokenized (same container tokens as Numerical/Text, empty children[]). Do not flag them as missing typography/text bindings.

trigger: FDS-Badge SB/Bet status/Won: fill NV var/fds/fds-sb-state-won but text NV var/fds/fds-sb-on-state-win (won vs win).
lesson: Sportsbook Won variant has a won/win NV naming asymmetry (fds-sb-state-won vs fds-sb-on-state-win). Not a binding error — do not rename during writes; flag for designer review.

trigger: FDS-Input audit: floating Label TEXT node has fill_ts=fds-on-surface-m but fill_nv=fds-on-surface-hi across all non-focus Surface states; in some states (Success/Alt) TS uses semantic state color fds-success on the label which is wrong.
lesson: For FDS-Input label text: TS fill says -m emphasis but NV is bound to -hi emphasis — systematic misalignment. Focus state aligns correctly (both hi). This is a known TS-NV drift on the floating label. Also: Never use semantic state tokens (fds-success/fds-error) as fill on label TEXT nodes — use the correct emphasis token.

trigger: FDS-Input tokens live on inner FRAME child named Input (depth 1), NOT on the COMPONENT root. Only itemSpacing is on the COMPONENT root. Matrix building from root node.ts gives all ∅.
lesson: For FDS-Input: COMPONENT root has only itemSpacing token. All fill/border/padding/radius/strokeWeight tokens are on the child FRAME named Input (depth 1). Use depth >= 2 or target named-child probes when auditing — root-level matrix will show all ∅ for fill/border.

trigger: FDS-Input readonly state: strokeWeight NV is bound (fds-stroke-150) but no borderColor TS key or strokes NV binding — border width set, border color unbound.
lesson: FDS-Input readonly: borderColor is intentionally absent (readonly has no visible border emphasis color — renders with transparent or default stroke). Verify with designer whether readonly should show fds-on-surface-ulow border or none.

trigger: FDS-Input COMPONENT root holds only itemSpacing; all fill/border/padding/radius/strokeWeight tokens live on child FRAME Input (depth 1). Root-level audit shows false-empty for fills/border.
lesson: For FDS-Input (and nested input-like components), audit the inner FRAME named Input at depth>=1 — the COMPONENT root only carries itemSpacing. Root-only token reads give false MISSING_* results.

ruleProposal.diff: # Add note: FDS-Input nests structural tokens on child FRAME Input (depth 1); root carries only itemSpacing. Probe inner frames by name.

trigger: FDS-Input floating Label TEXT: TS fill = fds-on-surface-m (or alt -m) but NV bound to fds-on-surface-hi (or alt -hi) on ALL non-focus variants; only Focus aligns both at hi. Success/Alt-Surf-Var label uses fds-success TS key (wrong category).
lesson: FDS-Input floating Label has systematic TS/NV emphasis drift (TS -m vs NV -hi) on every non-focus variant. Never use semantic state tokens (fds-success/fds-error) as label TEXT fill — use text-emphasis tokens. Flag as TS_NV_CONFLICT, do not auto-rebind.

trigger: FDS-Input readonly state: strokeWeight (fds-stroke-150) bound TS+NV but NO borderColor TS key and NO strokes NV — border drawn with weight but no color token.
lesson: FDS-Input readonly variants bind strokeWeight but have no borderColor token (TS or NV). Confirm with designer whether readonly border should bind fds-on-surface-ulow or render transparent before any write.

trigger: FDS-Input Default-Error COMPONENT root carries a stale borderColor:fds-error TS key with baked rawStrokes rgb(249,85,77); the live binding is on inner FRAME Input.
lesson: Watch for stale borderColor TS + rawStrokes on the COMPONENT root of Default-Error inputs — a copy-paste artifact on the wrong layer. The real binding is on FRAME Input; strip the root copy rather than binding it.

trigger: fds-collapsible chevron icon: on-surface uses fds-on-surface-m (medium emphasis) but on-alternate-surface uses fds-on-alternate-surface-low (low emphasis). Emphasis levels differ across themes for the same UI element.
lesson: For fds-collapsible chevron: on-surface=-m, on-alternate-surface=-low. This emphasis asymmetry should be flagged for designer review — if intentional (different visual weight on alternate surface) document it; if accidental, align to same emphasis level (-m or -low consistently).

trigger: fds-collapsible audit: all 8 COMPONENT roots have correct TS+NV fill/border, but chevron icon VECTORs (4), title TEXT nodes (2), and content-slot INSTANCEs (2) have TS only — zero NV bindings on any inner layer node.
lesson: For fds-collapsible: COMPONENT root tokens (fill, borderColor) are TS+NV complete. Inner nodes (chevron VECTORs, title TEXTs, content-slot INSTANCEs) are TS-only — NV binding pass required. Also: the on-alternate-surface Stroke=Hidden Accordion-State=Open content slot (8889:78084) carries fds-surface-variant fill TS instead of fds-alternate-surface-variant — copy-paste artifact. Fix before NV write pass.

ruleProposal.diff: # fds-collapsible inner nodes need NV write pass: chevron VECTORs (fds-on-surface-m / fds-on-alternate-surface-low), title TEXT (fds-on-surface-hi / fds-on-alternate-surface-hi + Paragraphs.fds typography), content slots (fds-surface-variant / fds-alternate-surface-variant). Fix 8889:78084 wrong fill token first.

trigger: Phase 3 bulk write dispatched to MIMR subagent — mcp_figma_use_figma write tool not available in VS Code Copilot subagent context; only read tools (get_design_context, get_metadata, get_variable_defs) are present. PAT also expired, blocking REST fallback.
lesson: mcp_figma_use_figma (Plugin API execution) is only available in the ODIN main session context, not in MIMR/VALI/SAGA subagent contexts. Phase 3 writes must be executed from ODIN directly (inject script + call mcp_figma_use_figma there), or the user must run the script manually in Figma. If dispatching Phase 3 as a subagent, include the write-tool invocation in ODIN before dispatching — do not dispatch the write execution itself.

ruleProposal.diff: # Add note to Phase 3 step 3: mcp_figma_use_figma is ODIN-only. For subagent dispatch of Phase 3, ODIN should run the Plugin API call itself using the script built by MIMR.

trigger: fds-sb-odds-button has 47 variants and no "Type" key, so the strict rule selects Sub-type B (flat). 47 flat instances are unwieldy to scan.
lesson: When Sub-type B (no Type key) would exceed ~20 variants, do NOT dump a flat grid. Offer to group by the lowest-value / highest-structure axis (e.g. Direction, Pre-built) as Sub-type A, and confirm with the user. Fell back to grouping by Direction (2 groups) for readability.

ruleProposal.diff: # Variant grid: if Sub-type B would exceed ~20 variants, group by a natural axis (Direction/Pre-built) A-style after user confirm, instead of a flat grid.

trigger: Flat/2-level variant grid too coarse for multi-axis sets; Usage was an empty placeholder while the component had a rich description.
lesson: Volundr Usage = component description lead paragraph (read set.description). For sets with >=3 axes or >20 variants use Sub-type C nested grid: Section=primary axis, Subsection=secondary axis, per-cell caption of remaining axes (read component.variantProperties, not name strings).

ruleProposal.diff: Added Usage=description rule + Sub-type C nested variant grid; mirrored in volundr.prompt.md and variant-parsing-rules.md

trigger: fds-sb-toggle alt-surface variants are light toggles designed for dark backgrounds; the page-template variant-grid background map (on-alternate-surface -> #ebebeb light) makes them near-invisible.
lesson: The dark artwork background rule must apply to the Variants grid Body and the Surfaces panels too, not only Anatomy: any group/cell whose Theme is an alternate/dark surface OR whose component is very light must render on the dark artwork background. Update page-template.md background-keyword map so on-alternate-surface (and light components) use artwork dark, not #ebebeb.

ruleProposal.diff: Variant-grid + Surfaces background: alternate-surface / very-light components use the dark artwork background (mirror anatomy-rules), not the light #ebebeb keyword fill.

trigger: The Section doc component clips a long Usage description — its inner text/placeholder frame is fixed-height, so a long lead paragraph overflows.
lesson: When overriding a Section instance with a long body (e.g. Usage description), set the Section description TEXT node textAutoResize=HEIGHT and grow its ancestor frames to HUG height, or the fixed-size component body clips the text.

ruleProposal.diff: Note in Generation model: after overriding Section body text, set the description node to auto-height + HUG ancestors so long Usage text does not clip.

trigger: Building doc-column sections with primaryAxisSizingMode=AUTO immediately followed by an explicit resize(w,h) call left several frames stuck at height=1 (content--control-props, section frames), clipping their own children and causing section--component below to overlap the truncated content.

ruleProposal.diff: Add to Build order: never resize() an AUTO-sized auto-layout frame before appending children; if height looks wrong after building, toggle primaryAxisSizingMode FIXED->AUTO to force relayout, then re-verify via get_metadata heights (not just a screenshot) before moving on.

lesson: When creating an AUTO-height auto-layout frame, do NOT call resize(w,h) right after setting sizing modes before children exist - it can pin the frame at the placeholder height. Append all children FIRST, then if a relayout is still stale, toggle the sizing mode off/on (e.g. set to FIXED then back to AUTO) to force Figma to recompute real height. Verify via get_metadata (not just screenshot) that container heights match their children's actual extent before trusting clipsContent.

trigger: Test-built fds-sb-toggle documentation in a brand-new test file (WK1o7C9Dd2qm9yOvUKqVsU) has the exact same component name as the already-archived fds-sb-toggle from a different file (rhSXN8LjWELGgCvtCnIxM6) - writing components/component/fds-sb-toggle.md would silently overwrite the real archive with test data.

ruleProposal.diff: Per-component archive: before writing components/component|widget/<name>.md, check if a file with that name already exists and belongs to a different fileKey/nodeId - if so, ask the user how to disambiguate (e.g. suffix by fileKey) instead of silently overwriting.

lesson: Per-component archive filenames must be namespaced by fileKey (or include it in the frontmatter and check it before overwriting) - component name alone is not a safe unique key across files. Skipped writing an archive for this test run to avoid corrupting the real fds-sb-toggle.md.
A full Volundr run must still build the Diagram+pins, not just the Legend list - the test build is an incomplete reference for that part specifically.
trigger: Test build only produced the Anatomy Legend (3 items) and skipped the actual reference-instance Diagram with numbered callout pins described in anatomy-rules.md, due to time/scope constraints of the test.
